data.title: Graphex Group Shareholders Approve All AGM Resolutions, Renew Capital Mandates
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An update from Graphex Group ADR ( (GRFXY) ) is now available.

Graphex Group Limited reported that all resolutions put to shareholders at its annual general meeting held on 29 June 2026 were approved by poll, including adoption of the 2025 audited financial statements and authorization for the board to set directors’ remuneration for 2026. Shareholders also backed the re-election of executive directors Chan Yick Yan Andross and Zhao Aiyong, the re-election of independent non-executive director Ren Chunyu, the reappointment of Crowe (HK) CPA Limited as auditor, and the renewal of share repurchase and issuance mandates, reinforcing the board’s authority over capital management and governance.

The company disclosed that 941,313,336 issued shares were eligible to vote, with no shareholders required to abstain and no reported opposition plans, and poll results showed strong majority support above 99% for most resolutions. The filing, signed by CEO Andross Chan on 1 July 2026, underscores Graphex’s continued compliance with Hong Kong listing requirements and signals broad shareholder confidence in the current leadership and its financial oversight framework.

Spark’s Take on GRFXY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GRFXY is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance—multi-year revenue contraction, sharply expanded losses, negative 2025 operating/free cash flow, and a much riskier balance sheet due to a spike in leverage. Technical indicators add limited support (neutral RSI but negative MACD and long-term downtrend versus 100/200-day averages), while valuation metrics are constrained by losses (negative P/E) and lack dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on GRFXY stock,
click here.

More about Graphex Group ADR

Graphex Group Limited is a foreign private issuer incorporated in the Cayman Islands, with its ordinary shares listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 6128 and its ADRs quoted on the OTC Expert Market. The company operates under Hong Kong listing rules and maintains a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical governance structure for a mid-cap HK-listed issuer.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Caledonia Mining ( (CMCL) ) has shared an update.

Caledonia Mining said on July 1, 2026 that it was notified by BlackRock, Inc. of a change in its shareholding position after the asset manager crossed a disclosure threshold on June 26, 2026 under AIM rules. BlackRock’s aggregate interest, including direct equity holdings and positions held through financial instruments such as securities lending and CFDs, rose to 6.12% of Caledonia’s voting rights, equivalent to 1,184,010 voting rights.

The filing reflects a modest increase from BlackRock’s previous reported stake of 5.98%, underscoring continued institutional investor engagement in Caledonia ahead of the summer reporting cycle. While the announcement does not alter Caledonia’s operations, it signals sustained interest from a major global asset manager, a factor that may support liquidity and market confidence in the company’s multi-listed shares.

data.title: Richmond Mutual Bancorporation Completes Farmers Bancorp Merger
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Richmond Mutual Bancorporation ( (RMBI) ) has shared an update.

Effective July 1, 2026, Richmond Mutual Bancorporation completed its stock-for-stock merger with The Farmers Bancorp, issuing approximately 6.25 million new shares and folding The Farmers Bank into First Bank Richmond, which was simultaneously rebranded as First Bank Midwest. The combined company retains the Richmond Mutual Bancorporation name, continues to trade under the Nasdaq ticker RMBI, and now operates all branches under the First Bank Midwest banner, with corporate headquarters in Richmond and bank headquarters in Frankfort, Indiana.

As part of the transaction, Richmond and First Bank Midwest expanded their boards from six to eleven members by adding five former Farmers directors, with former Farmers chair Barbara A. Cutillo named vice chair and Farmers’ former CEO Christopher D. Cook appointed president of Richmond and CEO of First Bank Midwest. Several other senior roles were filled by former Farmers executives, who entered into change-in-control agreements, underscoring a leadership integration aimed at leveraging complementary community banking markets and creating a larger regional franchise for employees, customers and shareholders.

Spark’s Take on RMBI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RMBI is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by improving operating performance with cash flow broadly backing earnings, but tempered by elevated leverage in recent annual filings and data inconsistencies in the latest-period balance sheet/cash flow. Technicals add support via a strong uptrend and positive MACD, while valuation is reasonable with a moderate P/E and attractive dividend yield. Recent merger approval and the quarterly dividend are constructive corporate catalysts.

To see Spark’s full report on RMBI stock,
click here.

More about Richmond Mutual Bancorporation

Richmond Mutual Bancorporation, Inc., headquartered in Richmond, Indiana, is the holding company for First Bank Midwest, a community-oriented financial institution. It offers traditional financial and trust services through a network of branches across multiple communities in Indiana and Ohio, focusing on serving local retail and small business customers.

Average Trading Volume: 50,556

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $166.8M

data.title: NewcelX Wins FDA Backing to Advance NCEL-101 Toward Type 1 Diabetes Trials
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

NewcelX ( (NCEL) ) has shared an update.

On July 1, 2026, NewcelX announced it had completed a successful Type B Pre-IND meeting with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, securing constructive feedback and alignment on its development strategy for NCEL-101 in type 1 diabetes. The FDA’s favorable view of the company’s chemistry, manufacturing and controls processes and preclinical program allows NewcelX to advance IND-enabling activities for a planned clinical trial of NCEL-101 combined with Eledon Pharmaceuticals’ investigational anti-CD40L antibody, tegoprubart.

The meeting outcome is a key milestone for NewcelX’s bid to establish an innovative treatment paradigm that could functionally cure type 1 diabetes by pairing enriched stem cell-derived islet cells with a calcineurin inhibitor-free immunosuppressive regimen. The collaboration leverages tegoprubart’s existing clinical transplant data, including supportive results from an investigator-initiated study in type 1 diabetes, potentially strengthening NewcelX’s position in cell-based diabetes therapies and offering stakeholders a clearer regulatory and clinical path forward.

More about NewcelX

NewcelX Ltd. is a clinical-stage regenerative medicine company focused on stem-cell-derived therapies for type 1 diabetes. Built on a human pluripotent stem cell platform, its lead program NCEL-101 aims to provide scalable, off-the-shelf islet cell replacement to restore functional insulin production and address significant unmet needs in diabetes care.

The company integrates cell therapy, immune protection and translational science to develop a comprehensive treatment approach. NewcelX is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker NCEL and operates from Switzerland, targeting a potential functional cure for type 1 diabetes through advanced islet cell therapy.

Average Trading Volume: 52,675

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $19.56M

data.title: Choice Hotels adds AI-focused leader to board
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

An update from Choice Hotels ( (CHH) ) is now available.

Choice Hotels International, Inc. is one of the largest global lodging franchisors, with more than 7,500 hotels and over 650,000 rooms across 51 countries and territories. Its portfolio of 22 brands spans full-service upper upscale, midscale, extended-stay and economy segments, supported by the Choice Privileges rewards program and co-branded credit cards aimed at driving value for travelers and franchise owners.

The company focuses on meeting diverse traveler needs while enhancing unit economics for franchisees and delivering returns to shareholders through its scalable franchising model and technology-enabled guest offerings.

Choice Hotels International appointed Ali Keshavarz to its Board of Directors effective June 26, 2026, expanding the board to twelve members and adding a seasoned leader in artificial intelligence and advanced analytics. Announced on July 1, 2026, the move underscores the company’s strategy to leverage AI and data to support long-term growth, improve hotel unit economics and enhance value for franchise owners, guests and shareholders.

Keshavarz, currently President and Chief Data & Analytics Officer at CVS Health and former McKinsey healthcare analytics leader, brings extensive experience in enterprise data, analytics and AI transformation, and will receive compensation consistent with other non-employee directors. His appointment signals Choice Hotels’ intent to deepen its technological capabilities and data-driven decision-making as competition intensifies across the lodging sector.

The company’s portfolio combines advanced construction techniques with intelligent water generation and automation platforms, positioning ModuLink at the intersection of smart cities, green building, and digital health technologies. data.description:

MindWalk Holdings ( (HYFT) ) has provided an announcement.

MindWalk Holdings has filed a new European patent application covering high-dimensional biological data structures that support its HYFT technology, ReefIQ context layer, and LensAI reasoning workflows, according to a July 1, 2026 announcement. The filing aims to protect a model-agnostic representation layer that organizes multi-modal biological context around recurring patterns, positioning the company to capture value as AI in drug discovery becomes increasingly dependent on domain-specific data architectures.

The patent builds on MindWalk’s existing HYFT estate by adding a reusable biological meaning layer designed to make AI-driven discovery more traceable, grounded, and reusable across customer programs. By anchoring its platform strategy in this context-rich data layer, already demonstrated in preclinical dengue and influenza programs and deployed with recurring LensAI customers, MindWalk seeks to strengthen its competitive position and create a scalable infrastructure asset as AI-enabled drug discovery spending grows.

data.title: Sify Technologies Files July 2026 Form 6-K, Furnishing FY 2025-26 Indian GAAP Annual Report
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Sify Technologies ( (SIFY) ) has issued an update.

On July 1, 2026, Sify Technologies Limited filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for the month of July 2026, furnishing its statutory annual report for fiscal year 2025-2026 prepared under Indian Accounting Standards as mandated by India’s Companies Act. The company noted that investors seeking its IFRS-compliant financials should refer to its Form 20-F annual report filed with the SEC on June 25, 2026, underscoring its dual-reporting framework for Indian and international stakeholders while clarifying that the 6-K materials are furnished rather than filed for Exchange Act liability purposes.

This dual disclosure approach highlights Sify’s effort to meet regulatory obligations in both India and the United States, potentially improving transparency and comparability for cross-border investors. By formally distinguishing between its Indian GAAP-equivalent statements and IFRS reporting, Sify aims to align with different accounting frameworks without expanding legal exposure under Section 18 of the Exchange Act, an important nuance for institutional shareholders and analysts tracking the company’s filings.

Spark’s Take on SIFY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SIFY is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by weak financial performance (net losses, rising leverage, and negative free cash flow from heavy capex). The earnings call provides a meaningful offset due to strong Data Center/Network momentum and contracted backlog, while technicals are neutral-to-soft and valuation is constrained by unprofitability and no dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on SIFY stock,
click here.

More about Sify Technologies

Sify Technologies Limited is an India-based technology company that operates as a foreign private issuer on U.S. markets, with its shares subject to reporting requirements under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The company prepares financial statements under both Indian Accounting Standards, in line with the Companies Act, 2013, and International Financial Reporting Standards for global investors.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Tortoise Energy Infrastructure Corp ( (TYG) ).

On July 1, 2026, Tortoise Energy Infrastructure Corporation implemented a planned board transition following the previously disclosed resignation of director Alexandra Herger, who had notified the company of her decision by letter dated April 24, 2026. The board appointed John Maxwell, a 63-year-old investment professional with decades of portfolio management and analyst experience and a Chartered Financial Analyst designation, to succeed Herger as a director and member of the Nominating and Governance Committee.

Maxwell, who retired in 2021 from his role as lead portfolio manager at Delaware Ivy Investments/Waddell & Reed/Ivy Investments and currently serves as an independent trustee for Tortoise Capital Series Trust, has been nominated to stand for election to a three-year term as a Class I director at the 2026 annual meeting. The company emphasized that Maxwell has no prior material relationship with Tortoise Energy Infrastructure Corporation or its adviser and will be compensated under the same 2025 director compensation framework that applies to other board members, underscoring a standard, independent governance structure for stakeholders.

More about Tortoise Energy Infrastructure Corp

Tortoise Energy Infrastructure Corporation is a closed-end investment company focused on energy infrastructure assets, providing investors with exposure to sectors such as midstream and related energy businesses. The company is advised by Tortoise Capital Advisors, L.L.C., and operates under a board-governed structure that emphasizes independent oversight and governance committees.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

DB Gold Double Long Exchange Traded Notes ( (DGP) ) has issued an update.

On June 30, 2026, Deutsche Bank reported key second-quarter updates, highlighting that it is exiting selected businesses and non-performing exposures to enhance shareholder value, including the sale of its retail, private banking and wealth management operations in India to Kotak Mahindra Bank. These actions are expected to raise second-quarter expenses and credit loss provisions by about €100 million each, but management says associated capital relief and balance sheet optimization should offset the impact over time.

The bank reiterated full-year 2026 cost guidance at slightly above €21 billion and confirmed plans for €0.9 billion of investments, while guiding to Q2 revenue growth in private banking and investment banking and a normalization of asset management fees. Management expressed confidence in achieving around €33 billion of group revenue in 2026 and maintaining the CET1 ratio in the mid-range of 13.5–14.0%, supporting a 60% payout policy and another share buyback, as Fitch upgraded Deutsche Bank’s long-term rating to A+ following strong issuance and capital actions.

More about DB Gold Double Long Exchange Traded Notes

Deutsche Bank AG is a global financial services institution headquartered in Frankfurt, operating across retail, private and wealth management, corporate banking, investment banking and asset management. It focuses on European and international markets, reporting under EU-endorsed IFRS with an emphasis on portfolio hedge accounting and value-accretive capital allocation.

On June 1, 2026, Mueller Industries, Inc. announced a two-for-one forward stock split of its issued common stock, to be implemented via an amendment to its Restated Certificate of Incorporation in Delaware. On June 29, 2026, the company filed this amendment, increasing its authorized common shares from 250 million to 500 million, with the change becoming effective at 4:01 p.m. Eastern Time that day.

Following these actions, Mueller Industries’ stock is set to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis at the market open on July 1, 2026, effectively doubling the number of shares outstanding while halving the price per share. This move is expected to enhance liquidity and broaden investor access to the stock, potentially supporting trading activity and reinforcing the company’s presence in the equity markets.

data.title: Valaris-Transocean deal advances with key U.S. approval
data.description:

Valaris ( (VAL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Valaris Limited and Transocean Ltd., both major players in offshore drilling, entered into a Business Combination Agreement on February 9, 2026, under which Transocean will acquire all issued and outstanding Valaris shares in an all-stock deal, offering 15.235 Transocean shares for each Valaris share. The combination, aimed at creating a larger offshore drilling group, is subject to multiple regulatory and shareholder approvals that will shape the companies’ future scale and competitive positioning in the sector.

On June 29, 2026, Valaris and Transocean secured approval from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States, satisfying a key regulatory condition for the deal. The transaction remains under review by the U.S. Department of Justice under the Hart-Scott-Rodino Act, with both companies responding to a Second Request and committing not to certify substantial compliance before July 31, 2026, while maintaining guidance that closing is expected in the second half of 2026, subject to remaining approvals and customary conditions.

The latest announcement is out from BranchOut Food Inc. ( (BOF) ).

BranchOut Food Inc. expanded its secured borrowing arrangements in 2026, building on a series of loans from Kaufman Kapital LLC that began with a $1.5 million senior secured promissory note on January 28, 2026, and were subsequently increased to $3 million by May 15, 2026. On June 30, 2026, the company further increased this facility by borrowing an additional $1 million under a Third Amended and Restated Secured Promissory Note totaling $4 million, maturing January 28, 2027 at 8% interest, with proceeds earmarked for working capital to produce customer orders and obligations secured by a lien on substantially all company assets, subject to customary covenants and default provisions.

The June 30, 2026 financing underscores BranchOut Food’s reliance on debt-funded working capital to support order production and highlights the company’s willingness to encumber most of its asset base to secure liquidity. For stakeholders, the enlarged, asset-backed credit facility suggests both increased operational funding capacity and heightened financial leverage, as the company operates under detailed affirmative and negative covenants and default triggers customary for secured lending in its sector.

Hub ( (HUBC) ) has provided an announcement.

On June 26, 2026, HUB Cyber Security Ltd. agreed to acquire all of certain investors’ senior subordinated convertible notes and purchase rights in Evofem Biosciences Inc., paying an aggregate purchase price of about $49.3 million in newly issued ordinary shares and pre-funded warrants. The acquired instruments could be converted or exercised into roughly 1.01 billion Evofem shares, potentially giving HUB about 88% ownership of Evofem’s current outstanding stock, while HUB shareholders must still approve full warrant exercise and an increase in authorized share capital.

To consummate the deal, HUB issued approximately 31.6 million consideration shares, structuring most of them as pre-funded warrants to keep individual holders below a 4.99% ownership cap, with plans to lift that limit after shareholder approval. The transaction, announced on July 1, 2026, forms part of a wider restructuring in which HUB has cut operating expenses, halved headcount at the HUB level and engaged Deloitte, positioning the Evofem investment as an initial disciplined step in pursuing strategic opportunities while preserving liquidity and seeking to enhance shareholder value.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from GreenPower Motor ( (GP) ).

On June 30, 2026, GreenPower Motor Company completed the third tranche of its Series A Convertible Preferred Share financing, issuing 1,500 preferred shares in a private placement for gross proceeds of US$1.425 million with an institutional investor under a previously established facility. On the same date, the company and the investor amended their agreement to increase the aggregate stated value of Series A Convertible Preferred Shares issuable by US$2 million, bolstering GreenPower’s funding capacity as it pursues its electric commercial vehicle strategy and supports ongoing operations.

Each Series A Convertible Preferred Share can be converted into common shares based on a formula tied to 105% of stated value and 125% of the prior day’s NASDAQ closing price, providing structured equity-linked financing while managing potential dilution. GreenPower will pay a 5% cash placement fee on the offering proceeds to Digital Offering LLC, underscoring the company’s continued use of capital markets and advisory channels to secure growth funding amid competitive pressure in the zero-emission commercial vehicle industry.
